title: Pledging Another $400k to the Zig Software Foundation
blurb: Mitchell Hashimoto pledged another $400,000 to the Zig Software Foundation, bringing his family's total support to $700,000 since an initial 2024 donation. The post defends ZSF's strict no-LLM contribution policy as a legitimate cultural boundary, framing the recent backlash around Bun's Zig fork as a failure of empathy rather than a fault in Zig's approach to maintainership and quality.

title: Lisp in the Rust Type System
blurb: A Rust project implements a Lisp interpreter entirely in the trait system, evaluating recursive functions, lexical scoping, and call/ec at compile time.

title: Leaving self-hosted Duende: what migrates, and what you stop operating
blurb: The post walks through migrating off self-hosted Duende IdentityServer, reading its SQL ConfigurationDb and ASP.NET Identity store to map clients, scopes, users, roles, and external providers onto a managed IdP. The key win is that ASP.NET Identity V3 and legacy bcrypt password hashes verify natively and rehash on first sign-in, so users skip resets, while the importer preserves user sub and client_id to keep refresh tokens and downstream SCIM rows intact.

title: The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE
blurb: Large DELETEs in Postgres add work rather than reclaim it: MVCC keeps dead tuples around, autovacuum only marks their space reusable instead of returning it to the OS, and the writes still replicate, stalling other writers under synchronous replication. The author argues that any schema expecting big cleanups should model deletion as DROP TABLE or TRUNCATE, which free disk immediately. Partition by the dimension you delete along, then drop partitions instead of scanning rows.

title: John Ternus set to re-establish importance of Apple’s design team when he takes over as CEO: report
blurb: Incoming Apple CEO John Ternus plans to restore the design team's executive authority, which eroded over the Cook era after Jony Ive's exit left oversight with operations and finance leaders, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. Apple currently has no senior design role, and Ternus has reportedly spent significant time with the industrial design group ahead of his September 1 start and a fall foldable iPhone launch.

title: Using Scroll-Driven Animations for Opposing Scroll Directions
blurb: A CSS-Tricks tutorial walks through building columns of items that scroll in opposing directions using scroll-driven animations, with a CSS-only mask effect that fades items as they cross the container edges. The approach relies on modern `animation-timeline` features, so it targets larger screens, respects reduced-motion settings, and currently works only in Chrome and Safari.

title: Atonemo and NTS launch Radio Player with icon wheel for genre selection
blurb: Atonemo and NTS released the Atonemo NTS Radio Player, a pocket-sized wireless streamer that plugs into any speaker and uses a physical icon wheel to pick genres rather than a screen or search field. The design leans on "omakase listening," handing curation to NTS hosts instead of the listener, a deliberate counter to algorithmic feeds and infinite-scroll music interfaces.

title: NSA director: 'Mythos "broke into almost all of our classified systems in hours"
blurb: The NSA director told The Economist that Anthropic's Mythos model "broke into almost all of our classified systems in hours," a disclosure that surfaced amid the Trump administration's move to block the company from federal work. The account underscores how far offensive AI tooling has advanced inside government red-team exercises, even as the legal fight over Anthropic's access to classified networks remains unresolved.

title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
blurb: Microsoft attributed last week's Mastra NPM supply chain attack to North Korean group Sapphire Sleet (BlueNoroff), which compromised the 'ehindero' maintainer account on June 17 and published 141 packages depending on easy-day-js, a dayjs typosquat carrying an obfuscated postinstall dropper. Any workstation or CI/CD pipeline that ran npm install during the 45-minute window should be treated as compromised, given the affected @mastra packages pull roughly 8M weekly downloads.

title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data
blurb: Squidbleed (CVE-2026-47729), a Heartbleed-style memory leak that has sat in Squid Proxy's FTP parser since 1997, lets an attacker controlling a reachable FTP server read past a buffer boundary into uncleared HTTP request data from other users, exposing credentials, session tokens, and API keys in shared proxy environments. Exposure is limited to cleartext HTTP and TLS-terminating deployments; patches shipped in Squid 8 and 7.6, and disabling unused FTP support mitigates the risk.

title: Hundreds of AI-powered iOS apps found exposing credentials
blurb: Researchers at Wake Forest University analyzed 444 iOS apps with LLM features and found 282 (64%) exposing exploitable credentials, including 136 leaking authentication tokens, 92 allowing unauthenticated backend access, and 54 hardcoding plaintext API keys. The leakage was systemic rather than niche, affecting apps with up to 2.3 million ratings, and 55% routed traffic through custom developer backends that provider-side mitigations alone cannot fix.

title: Ethereum Layer 2 Taiko halts block production following exploit; urges users to withdraw funds
blurb: Taiko, an Ethereum Layer 2, halted block production after an exploit and urged users to withdraw funds, with onchain security firm Blockaid attributing the likely root cause to a flaw in the Taiko bridge's source-signal proof validation. The halt freezes the rollup while the validation bug is investigated, so users with assets bridged through Taiko should exit to Ethereum mainnet before block production resumes.

title: PixelSmash – Critical FFmpeg Vulnerability Turns Media Files into Weapons
blurb: JFrog disclosed PixelSmash (CVE-2026-8461, CVSS 8.8), a heap out-of-bounds write in FFmpeg's MagicYUV decoder that escalates from a crash to reliable RCE when an application processes a single crafted media file. Researchers demonstrated full code execution against Jellyfin and Nextcloud via a 50 KB AVI upload; upgrade FFmpeg now, or disable the MagicYUV decoder at build time if it isn't needed.

title: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
blurb: Cybercrime group Icarus breached Vancouver-based market intelligence provider Klue on June 12 using a compromised legacy credential tied to an integration tool, stealing customer Salesforce data from firms including HackerOne, Snyk, Recorded Future, Tanium, Jamf, and OneTrust. Icarus has threatened to publish the stolen records, mostly business contact details, on Monday absent a ransom payment, making Klue the latest middleware provider hit in a wave of single-point-of-failure supply-chain attacks alongside Gainsight and Salesloft.

title: Taiko halts its Ethereum layer-2 network after a bridge exploit, token dives
blurb: An attacker forged cross-chain withdrawal proofs to drain about $1.7 million from Taiko's bridge, prompting the Ethereum layer-2 network to halt block production and urge users to withdraw funds. BlockSec traced the likely root cause to a Raiko prover signing key left publicly exposed on GitHub, which let the attacker enroll fraudulent provers and register fake withdrawals on Ethereum without matching deposits on Taiko.

title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
blurb: Canada's Federal Court has disclosed that CSIS used a threat reduction warrant for the first time to reach into Canadian-soil servers, SOHO routers, and IoT devices and dismantle two foreign-run botnets, altering and destroying the malicious data without seeking user identities or intercepting content. The court found the threat clearly established and imminent, flagging the energy sector as a target, though the redacted ruling does not attribute the operators beyond foreign-state actors.

title: How Claude Mythos found a 15-year-old bug in Mozilla Firefox | Brian Grinstead
blurb: Mozilla distinguished engineer Brian Grinstead detailed the agentic harness behind Firefox's record month of security fixes, including a 15-year-old bug, and argues the pipeline deserves roughly half the credit versus Anthropic's Mythos model. Key components: an LLM judge that scores and ranks files before spending compute, a verifier subagent that kills false positives, and a goal-loop that retries scoped problems past the point a human quits. Humans still review every patch.

title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
blurb: Paradigm Shift disclosed Usbliter8, an unpatchable BootROM exploit in Apple's SecureROM that chains a USB controller bug and a firmware config weakness to trigger an out-of-bounds write and gain full code execution before the OS loads on A12/A13 iPhones and S4/S5 Apple Watches. The attack requires physical USB access and cannot read user data directly since the Secure Enclave stays intact, but PoC code is public and the affected chips cannot be fixed via software, making it most useful to forensics vendors.

title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign
blurb: Fortinet says the FortiBleed campaign harvesting credentials from its firewalls and VPNs exploits no new vulnerability, instead reusing credentials from prior breaches and brute-forcing devices lacking MFA, yielding 86,000 working credentials across 194 countries. Compromised FortiGate customers should terminate admin and VPN sessions, rotate credentials, enforce MFA, and upgrade to releases supporting PBKDF2 hashing of administrator credentials.

title: Encrypted DNS still tells an eavesdropper where to look
blurb: Encrypted DNS over TLS, HTTPS, QUIC, and CoAP hides query contents but leaves plaintext headers that mark a flow as DNS, letting an eavesdropper on a wireless link single it out to block, profile, or analyze it. A new study trained a Random Forest classifier on 296 IoT scenarios and found addresses, ports, and monotonic counters leak the most. The authors propose eliding headers via SCHC compression and CoAP block-wise transfer to even out packet lengths.

title: 23 ClawHub plugins squatting official scopes expose AI registry security gaps
blurb: Researchers found 23 code-executing plugins published under ClawHub's official @openclaw and @clawhub scopes while owned by unrelated accounts, since the registry never reserved those namespaces to their legitimate owners. Even with non-malicious code, the official-looking scopes are a supply-chain risk that could let an attacker push trusted-seeming plugins to Claude and OpenClaw agents; ClawHub restricted scope ownership after the disclosure.

title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
blurb: Elastic Security Labs disclosed REF8372, a financially motivated campaign that uses malicious Google Ads impersonating a verified Ukrainian advertiser to deliver CastleStealer via a new loader dubbed OXLOADER. Victims searching for software like Node.js are redirected to fake sites serving Storj-hosted batch scripts that fetch OXLOADER, which uses control-flow flattening, mixed Boolean-Arithmetic, and DLL side-loading to evade static detection and deploy the .NET stealer.

title: Brazil probes emergency warning system after nationwide rogue alert
blurb: Brazil's government opened an investigation into its national emergency warning system after a rogue alert was pushed to devices across the country, pointing to a compromise or abuse of the alert-distribution infrastructure. Operators of mass-notification and cell-broadcast systems should audit access controls and authentication on alert-origination endpoints, since unauthorized message injection undermines public trust and can be weaponized for panic or follow-on phishing.

title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
blurb: ShinyHunters' latest wave of breaches (University of Nottingham, DentaQuest, 7-Eleven, Medtronic, Wynn Resorts) shows the group rarely exploits software flaws, instead abusing identity: infostealer-harvested credentials, OAuth token theft, MFA fatigue, vishing, help desk impersonation, and overly permissive SaaS and guest-access configs across Salesforce, Snowflake, and Okta. Defenders should enforce phishing-resistant MFA, audit OAuth grants and guest-user permissions, and monitor for anomalous authentication rather than relying on perimeter controls.

title: Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals
blurb: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department disclosed a breach affecting roughly 3 million people who bought hunting and fishing licenses, stemming from a cyberattack on an unnamed third-party license-sales vendor. Exposed data includes email and physical addresses, phone numbers, driver's license details, and passport numbers, but not Social Security numbers, dates of birth, or financial information.

title: Agent Beacon: Open-source telemetry layer for AI agents
blurb: Asymptote Labs released Beacon, an open-source (MIT, mostly Go) telemetry layer that configures OpenTelemetry export and hooks for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Cursor, then normalizes their file edits, command execution, tool use, and approval decisions into a single JSON event log. It runs locally without an account, binds its dashboard to loopback, offers redaction and retention modes, and can forward events into customer-managed SIEMs.

title: In a rare joint statement, Five Eyes leaders warn AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses are mere months away, urging leaders to "act now" (Sarah Basford Canales/The Guardian)
blurb: Five Eyes intelligence leaders issued a rare joint statement warning that AI models capable of disabling governments and businesses are months away, urging policymakers to "act now." The statement signals likely near-term regulatory and procurement shifts for security teams, though it offers no specifics on capabilities, timelines, or the controls defenders should put in place.

title: Gizmodo readers hit with ClickFix malware prompts after account compromise
blurb: Attackers compromised a Gizmodo account to serve site visitors fake verification prompts that use the ClickFix technique, tricking readers into pasting malicious commands into the Windows Run dialog to self-infect with malware. Defenders should block clipboard-to-Run execution paths via attack-surface-reduction rules, hunt for PowerShell or mshta launches spawned from explorer.exe, and warn users that legitimate sites never ask them to paste commands to prove they are human.

title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
blurb: Google will begin enforcing Android developer verification on September 30, 2026, blocking normal installs of unverified apps on certified devices in Brazil, Indonesia, Singapore, and Thailand across Google Play and OEM stores from Samsung, Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo, Honor, and Transsion. Developers must register a legal identity and sign each app to stay installable; unregistered apps will only reach certified phones via ADB or Google's 24-hour high-friction sideload flow, hitting independent and open-source channels hardest.

title: Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data
blurb: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS 5.3), a sensitive-information-exposure flaw in the Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin before version 2.1.5, hitting an unauthenticated REST API endpoint that returns the full system report including configured email API keys and OAuth tokens. Defiant has blocked over 17 million attempts since exploitation began in early May; admins should update to 2.1.5, audit access logs, and rotate any exposed third-party SMTP credentials.

title: Cisco widens agentic security with AI identity acquisition
blurb: Cisco announced its intent to acquire WideField Security, a 2023-founded identity-security startup, integrating its technology into the Splunk suite to strengthen agentic SOC capabilities by correlating identity, session, and activity telemetry across human, machine, and AI-agent activity. The deal extends a buying streak that includes Galileo Technologies and Astrix Security, positioning Cisco to sell unified machine- and agent-identity context as autonomous workloads proliferate.

title: Professional Athletes and Wearables
blurb: Wearable biometric data collected from professional athletes is emerging as a serious privacy and labor concern, with leagues eyeing commercialization and bettors wanting access to data like a player's overnight heart rate or sleep timing. The author notes the unusual dynamic: unlike most surveillance tech deployed first against people with diminished rights, the surveilled here are wealthy, powerful, and often unionized, with aging and injured players most exposed during contract negotiations.

title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
blurb: Flare analyzed 470 underground forum posts from January 2025 to June 2026 advertising "search your target" services, a dedicated layer that lets buyers query infostealer databases of tens of billions of lines for credentials matching a specific company, domain, platform, or geography instead of buying bulk combo lists. The brokers sit between infostealer infections and account takeover, offering filtering, deduplication, and formatting into outputs like URL:LOGIN:PASS and MAIL:PASS. Buyer feedback flags a gap between advertised and actual quality, with lower volumes and many invalid or duplicated credentials.

title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data
blurb: Researchers at Calif.io disclosed Squidbleed (CVE-2026-47729), a Heartbleed-style memory leak in the Squid open source proxy that has persisted since 1997 and can expose other users' cleartext HTTP request data, including credentials, session tokens, and API keys. The bug is patched in Squid 7.6 and 8 and matters most in shared-proxy environments like corporate networks and public Wi-Fi, where IT teams should patch or disable FTP support.

title: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
blurb: Hackers breached Vancouver-based market intelligence vendor Klue on June 12 using a compromised legacy credential, then pulled customer data from connected Salesforce clouds at firms including Gong, Jamf, HackerOne, Snyk, Recorded Future, and Tanium. The breach extends a run of supply-chain attacks on middleware integrators like Gainsight and Salesloft, raising the procurement question of how much third-party data access enterprises grant tools that link directly into their cloud databases.

title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
blurb: Elastic Security Labs disclosed REF8372, a financially motivated campaign that uses malicious Google Ads impersonating a verified Ukraine-based advertiser to deliver the CastleStealer infostealer through a new loader dubbed OXLOADER. The loader hosts payloads on Storj to evade domain-reputation filters, uses DLL side-loading and heavy obfuscation to bypass static detection, and excludes CIS machines, pointing to a likely Russian-speaking operator.

title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign
blurb: Fortinet says the FortiBleed campaign harvesting credentials from its firewalls and VPNs exploits no new vulnerability, instead reusing leaked credentials and brute-forcing accounts with weak passwords and no MFA, with attackers compiling 86,000 working credentials across 194 countries. Customers running exposed FortiGate instances should rotate credentials, enforce MFA on all admin and VPN accounts, and upgrade to releases supporting PBKDF2 password hashing.

title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
blurb: Samsung Electronics deployed ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, making it one of OpenAI's largest enterprise rollouts to date. The deal embeds OpenAI's models and coding agent directly into a global manufacturer's workflows, expanding OpenAI's enterprise footprint against Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini in accounts where those incumbents already hold ground.

title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
blurb: European research firm Paradigm Shift disclosed Usbliter8, an unpatchable BootROM exploit in Apple's SecureROM that grants full code execution before the OS loads on iPhones with A12 and A13 chips, including the XS, XR, and 11, plus S4 and S5 Apple Watches. The flaw requires physical USB access and cannot reach Secure Enclave-protected user data, but it hands forensics vendors a durable unlock path on millions of 2018-2019 devices that no patch can close.

title: Apple Price Increases, Apple Intelligence and the E.U.
blurb: Apple is raising prices on its hardware lineup while declining to ship Apple Intelligence's Siri features to the E.U., citing regulatory friction under the Digital Markets Act. The split signals that Apple will treat the E.U. as a second-tier market for AI rollout, leaving enterprise fleets there on older Siri capabilities even as device costs climb.

title: Brazil probes emergency warning system after nationwide rogue alert
blurb: Brazil's telecom regulator opened an investigation after the country's emergency warning system pushed a rogue alert to phones nationwide, raising questions about whether the platform was breached or misconfigured. The probe puts scrutiny on the integrity controls behind government cell-broadcast infrastructure, a system enterprises and the public increasingly rely on for authenticated crisis notifications.

title: Gizmodo readers hit with ClickFix malware prompts after account compromise
blurb: Attackers compromised Gizmodo accounts to serve readers ClickFix prompts that trick them into pasting malicious commands into Windows Run, installing malware. The incident shows how a publisher's hijacked CMS or ad supply becomes a malware distribution channel, raising the bar for vetting third-party content and ad partners across any media-adjacent web property.

title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
blurb: ShinyHunters' recent breaches at the University of Nottingham, DentaQuest, 7-Eleven, Medtronic, and Wynn Resorts followed a consistent identity-centric playbook, abusing stolen credentials, OAuth tokens, MFA fatigue, vishing, and overly permissive SaaS configurations across Salesforce, Snowflake, and Okta rather than exploiting software flaws. The pattern tells security leaders to shift spend from perimeter defense to identity governance, MFA enforcement, and anomalous-authentication monitoring across their SaaS stack.

title: Tesco races to migrate 40,000 servers away from VMware amid licensing spat
blurb: Tesco is migrating roughly 40,000 servers off VMware "at exceptional pace," targeting completion by end of 2027, after filing a 2025 breach-of-contract suit alleging Broadcom failed to honor perpetual licenses it bought in 2021 for vSphere Foundation, Cloud Foundation, and Tanzu. The retailer seeks $130M in damages and says it has been forced into costlier alternatives with reduced functionality, marking one of the largest post-Broadcom VMware exits to date.

title: Microsoft tells Windows users to get ready for 26H2 – unless they're on 26H1
blurb: Microsoft told Windows customers to begin preparing for the 26H2 feature update, while carving out an exception for systems already running 26H1, which stay on their current servicing track. The split messaging complicates upgrade planning for IT teams managing mixed fleets, who now have to map which devices target 26H2 and which sit on 26H1 before scheduling deployment rings.

title: Copilot Cowork is now generally available: Everything you need to know, including pricing, usage limits, and new features
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide, integrating Anthropic's Claude Cowork into Microsoft 365 Copilot with new model choice, plugin extensibility, and cost-management controls. The launch hands enterprises a task-automating agent that can manage files and send emails on a worker's behalf, and its arrival has already pressured dedicated SaaS vendors whose functions it threatens to absorb.

title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
blurb: Google will begin enforcing Android developer verification on September 30, 2026, blocking normal app installs from unregistered developers on certified devices in Brazil, Indonesia, Singapore, and Thailand, across both Google Play and OEM stores run by Samsung, Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo, Honor, and Transsion. With certified devices covering 95% of Android outside China, IT teams distributing internal or open-source apps in those markets must register a legal identity and signed APK with Google or lose new-install access.

title: AI 도입 넘어 비용 관리로…오픈AI, 사용량 분석·예산 설정 기능 공개
blurb: OpenAI added spend controls and enhanced usage analytics to ChatGPT Enterprise, giving admins a centralized Global Admin Console that tracks ChatGPT and Codex credit consumption by user, product, and model, plus per-team budgets. The tooling answers enterprise demand for AI cost governance as agent sprawl grows, though Forrester and Gartner analysts note it measures activity rather than tying spend to business outcomes.

title: WhatsApp head Will Cathcart is stepping down after seven years
blurb: Meta replaced WhatsApp head Will Cathcart, who led the app for seven years, with Cred founder Kunal Shah and is investing $900M into the Indian fintech for a 20% stake. The appointment signals Meta's intent to push WhatsApp deeper into payments and commerce, with India as the proving ground for the monetization features Cathcart began with ads and AI chat.

title: Lessons from the VMwars – nothing virtual about the Broadcom vs Tesco slugfest
blurb: The author dissects the legal fight between Broadcom and UK retailer Tesco over VMware licensing terms, using it to draw lessons for any enterprise still running virtualized infrastructure post-acquisition. The core argument is that Broadcom's shift to subscription bundles and aggressive audit posture leaves customers with little leverage once they are locked in. The practical takeaway for IT leaders is to model migration costs and contractual exit terms before the next renewal, not after the invoice arrives.

title: Cisco widens agentic security with AI identity acquisition
blurb: Cisco agreed to acquire WideField Security and fold its identity, session, and activity telemetry into Splunk's agentic SOC, building on its earlier Galileo and Astrix deals. The buy extends Cisco's push to give Splunk customers unified context across human, machine, and AI-agent activity, deepening the Cisco Data Fabric as a security layer for monitoring autonomous workloads at scale.

title: Free Software Foundation Europe pushes EU to force Google to allow AI uninstalls on Android
blurb: The Free Software Foundation Europe asked the European Commission to force Google to let Android users fully uninstall pre-loaded AI models and to decouple interoperability from Google's developer-verification requirements, citing DMA violations after Google's AI components silently reinstalled when deleted. The submission targets Google's Android Developer Certification, due September 2026, which would require every developer to register, sign contracts, and pay account fees to publish apps even on alternative stores like F-Droid.

title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
blurb: Flare researchers analyzed 470 underground forum posts from January 2025 to June 2026 and documented a "search your target" market, where buyers query brokers for credentials matching a specific company, domain, or geography rather than buying bulk combo lists. The service layer sits between infostealer infections and account takeover, though buyer feedback shows advertised volumes are often inflated and many extracted credentials are invalid or duplicated.

title: Chevron signs a 20-year deal with Microsoft to provide natural-gas power for a proposed West Texas data center, which could be one of the biggest in the US (Kevin Crowley/Bloomberg)
blurb: Chevron signed a 20-year deal to supply natural-gas power to Microsoft for a proposed West Texas data center that could rank among the largest in the US. The agreement locks in long-term generation capacity as Microsoft races to secure power for AI buildout, signaling that gas, not just renewables or nuclear, remains central to hyperscaler infrastructure planning.

title: 2003-era DDR2 memory prices jump up to 60% — AI-driven DRAM shortage reaches the oldest standard still in production
blurb: DDR2 contract prices rose 55% to 60% in Q2 and TrendForce projects another 35% to 40% in Q3, as the AI-driven DRAM shortage cascades down to a standard that first shipped in 2003. With Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron steering wafers toward HBM and server DRAM, buyers are redesigning embedded, networking, industrial, and automotive systems around older memory to secure supply, raising bill-of-materials costs for long-lived hardware.

title: Microsoft is building an AI datacenter that "uses less water than a fast food restaurant"
blurb: Microsoft is building project Kilby, a 2GW Texas AI datacenter powered by a 20-year Chevron natural-gas deal and closed-loop cooling.

title: Microsoft accidentally kills epic Outlook email threads
blurb: Microsoft introduced an Outlook bug that broke conversation threading, scattering long email chains into individual disconnected messages for affected users. The defect hits IT teams that rely on Outlook's grouped view to triage high-volume mailboxes and shared inboxes, adding helpdesk load until Microsoft ships a fix through its standard update channel.

title: Agent Beacon: Open-source telemetry layer for AI agents
blurb: Asymptote Labs released Beacon, an open-source Go tool that configures OpenTelemetry export for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Cursor, normalizing their file edits, command runs, and tool calls into a single JSON event log readable from a loopback dashboard or forwarded to a SIEM. It runs locally under MIT license with no account required, defaulting to full capture but offering redacted retention modes that strip prompt text, command output, and raw diffs.

title: Telemetry that matters: Designing sustainable, high-impact observability pipelines
blurb: A panel at Observability Summit North America argued that "instrument everything, filter later" is a failed default, citing that roughly 50% of collected metrics are never queried or acted on. The waste inflates storage bills, alert noise, and on-call cognitive load during incidents, and carries a real compute and energy cost the panel frames as "green observability." Their fix is to treat observability as a day-zero design requirement: define what healthy looks like, then collect only the OpenTelemetry signals that detect drift.

title: TikTok Adds Post-Publish Keyword Management Tool for Creator Videos
blurb: TikTok's new "Manage Keywords" tool lets creators add or block search keywords on already-published videos, giving marketers a post-publish lever on algorithmic distribution.

title: Amazon Prime Day Spending Expected to Rise 9% to $26.3 Billion
blurb: Amazon Prime Day spending is expected to rise 9% to a record $26.3 billion during the 12th annual sale beginning Tuesday, with back-to-school clothes and household goods driving the bulk of online purchases. Higher gas prices are pushing deal-seeking shoppers toward the event, giving marketers a concentrated demand window to time promotions, retail media spend, and competing flash sales against.

title: Instagram looks to take on streaming services with longer-form, episodic and live formats for its TV app
blurb: Instagram is expanding its TV app with longer-form video, episodic series, and live creator formats, positioning it against Netflix and Amazon Prime Video as it pushes into living-room viewing. The app is rolling out to Samsung TVs alongside existing Amazon Fire and Google TV support, and adding personalized channels, phone-to-TV casting, and horizontal video to capture creator audiences on the largest screen in the house.

title: Getty Images Strikes Deal with OpenAI, Sending Getty’s Stock Soaring
blurb: Getty Images and OpenAI signed a multi-year deal to surface Getty's licensed visual content inside ChatGPT's search and discovery experiences, sending Getty's stock up more than 200% in premarket trading before settling around 123%. The terms stay vague on money, scope, and usage rights, but the move signals OpenAI is paying for licensed imagery to make AI answers more trustworthy after years of publishers fighting unlicensed training.

title: Rippling’s AI Bet: The Data Graph Is the Moat
blurb: Rippling's AI product lead argues that the company's real moat isn't the model but the single connected employee graph beneath all 25+ products, built in-house rather than acquired and stitched together. The demo walked a three-stage arc that maps to how any team should build AI on business data: insights first (a top-performer report surfaced that 71% of top performers had six-plus years of tenure), then actions, then proactive workflows. The harder problem is the middle layer that handles field relationships and permissions, which is where accuracy and trust come from.

title: Ethereum Layer 2 Taiko halts block production following exploit; urges users to withdraw funds
blurb: Taiko, an Ethereum Layer 2, halted block production after an exploit and urged users to withdraw funds from the network. Onchain security firm Blockaid attributed the likely root cause to a flaw in the Taiko bridge's source-signal proof validation, raising questions about cross-chain message integrity on optimistic rollups.

title: OKX and NYSE partner to bridge tradfi and crypto markets in joint venture led by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: OKX and Intercontinental Exchange formed a joint venture, led by former New York governor Andrew Cuomo, to give OKX's 120 million users access to ICE futures and NYSE tokenized equities. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US-registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, extending ICE's digital-asset push following its strategic investment in OKX at a $25B valuation.

title: Ethereum validators asked to fund projects with up to 10% of staking rewards under new proposal
blurb: A new Ethereum governance proposal called "validator redirected revenue" would let validators redirect 0% to 10% of their staking rewards toward shared ecosystem infrastructure and public goods, with funds routed through a splitter contract. If a majority of validators signal support for a nonzero rate, the contribution becomes mandatory for all, potentially channeling tens of thousands of ETH annually into projects that address the network's free-rider problem.

title: Japan’s corporate pension fund plans to allocate 1% of assets to crypto: reports
blurb: Japan's National Business Corporate Pension Fund plans to allocate roughly 1% of its 21.3 billion yen ($131.8M) in assets to crypto starting in fiscal 2026, investing through a passive fund run by a hedge fund. The move accompanies a broader currency-diversification shift that cuts the fund's yen weighting from 80% to 70%, and lands as Japan advances a bill to classify crypto as financial instruments.

title: Taiko halts its Ethereum layer-2 network after a bridge exploit, token dives
blurb: Taiko halted block production on its Ethereum layer-2 network and told users to withdraw funds after an attacker forged cross-chain proofs to drain about $1.7 million from its bridge. The TAIKO token has fallen more than 20% since midnight UTC, and security firm BlockSec traced the likely cause to a Raiko signing key left publicly accessible on GitHub.

title: Bank of England drops proposed holding caps for sterling stablecoins, sets £40 billion issuance guardrail
blurb: The Bank of England dropped proposed individual holding caps for sterling stablecoins and set a £40B systemic issuance guardrail, with revised backing-asset rules targeting a 2027 launch. The reversal removes a per-user balance ceiling that issuers had warned would cap adoption, shifting the constraint to aggregate supply rather than wallet-level limits.

title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
blurb: North Korean state-sponsored group Sapphire Sleet compromised an NPM maintainer account and pushed 141 malicious Mastra packages on June 17, slipping a typosquatted dependency into a framework with roughly 8 million weekly downloads, Microsoft reported. The postinstall payload ran during npm install on Windows, macOS, and Linux, harvesting system data and targeting over 160 cryptocurrency browser extensions to drain wallets.

title: MoneyGram joins Solana as validator amid stablecoin payment push
blurb: MoneyGram became a validator on Solana, helping process and secure transactions on the proof-of-stake network as it expands its stablecoin payments strategy. The move follows the launch of its MGUSD stablecoin on Stellar earlier this month and an anchor-validator role on payments chain Tempo, signaling a multi-chain approach to building remittance rails on open networks.

title: Polymarket paid creators to stage fake winning bets on dummy sites: WSJ
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that Polymarket paid influencers to record hype videos showing fake winning bets placed on dummy versions of the prediction-market site. The Journal reviewed more than 1,100 videos and concluded that none of the roughly $1.9 million in wagers displayed were real, raising fresh scrutiny of the platform's marketing as it pushes for US expansion.

title: Bitcoin developers want to fix the 'replace this transaction with a higher fee' button. Here's why
blurb: Bitcoin Core developers are proposing to remove explicit replace-by-fee (RBF) signaling from wallet software, since full-RBF is now the network's default policy and the opt-in flag has become redundant. Keeping the legacy signal leaves an on-chain fingerprint that reveals which wallet sent a transaction, so developers are coordinating on a shared default sequence number, likely the already dominant MAX-2, to make transactions across wallets indistinguishable.

title: Bitget enables direct US stock purchases using crypto
blurb: Bitget launched Stock+, a feature that lets users buy full and fractional US stocks funded directly with crypto through regulated brokers. The product routes orders to licensed brokerages, bringing equity exposure onto the exchange without requiring users to convert holdings to fiat first.

title: Cuomo to Lead Crypto Venture With New York Stock Exchange Owner
blurb: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo will lead a new venture from OKX and Intercontinental Exchange, the owner of the New York Stock Exchange, aimed at merging digital-asset and traditional finance markets. The tie-up pairs a major crypto exchange operator with one of the largest regulated US market infrastructure firms, signaling deeper institutional convergence between the two systems.

title: South Korea’s Toss Bank to test blockchain-based financial infrastructure on Solana
blurb: Toss Bank, South Korea's internet-only bank with 15 million customers, signed an MOU with the Solana Foundation to build blockchain financial infrastructure, starting with a proof-of-concept for stablecoin-based overseas remittances and settlement. The first phase verifies technical feasibility of Solana-powered transfers before later phases add overseas partners and AML/KYC checks across Toss's existing 30-country, seven-currency remittance network.

title: Ethereum ‘tax’ debate erupts over proposal to redirect portion of staking rewards to ecosystem funding
blurb: An Ethereum Research forum post proposes letting validators opt in to redirect up to 10% of their staking rewards toward funding public goods across the ecosystem. The idea has split the community, with critics framing any reduction in validator yield as a de facto tax that could push stakers toward chains with higher net returns.

title: Bank of England backs down on strict stablecoin holding limits, sets $50 billion issuance cap
blurb: The Bank of England abandoned its proposed retail and corporate stablecoin holding limits, replacing them with a temporary £40 billion ($50.6 billion) issuance cap on any single systemic stablecoin and cutting required non-interest-bearing central bank reserves to 30%. Issuers can now hold up to 70% of backing in short-term UK gilts, though direct interest payments to holders remain banned ahead of full UK crypto rules in 2027.

title: Tokenized RWA market cap rises 40% to top $51 billion as industry races to define equity tokenization model: Bernstein
blurb: Tokenized real-world assets reached a $51B market cap, up 40%, with tokenized equities growing 130% as issuers compete to set the dominant model, according to Bernstein. The race centers on which structure wins out, from direct on-chain shares to wrapped derivatives, as firms position for a market still defining its core legal and custody mechanics.

title: TradFi fund manager Baillie Gifford introduces Solana, Ethereum tokenized fund with BNY
blurb: Baillie Gifford launched the Baillie Gifford Enhanced Yield Fund (BAGEY), a dollar-denominated tokenized fund of short-duration corporate bonds issued natively on Ethereum and Solana, with BNY providing tokenization and wallet infrastructure and NatWest serving as depositary. The fund runs through a UK-regulated OEIC structure, currently yields around 7%, and opens to eligible investors in the UK, Switzerland, and the Cayman Islands.

title: Altura winds down stablecoin vault after ‘unprecedented level’ of withdrawal requests
blurb: Altura wound down its stablecoin vault after what the company called an unprecedented level of withdrawal requests, with its CEO attributing the run partly to market speculation. The exit follows contagion fears tied to the depeg of Main Street's msUSD, underscoring how quickly redemption pressure can cascade across linked stablecoin and yield-vault products.

title: Crypto trading app Fomo raises $75 million at $550 million valuation in Index Ventures-led Series B
blurb: Fomo raised $75M in a Series B at a $550M valuation, led by Index Ventures with participation from Union Square Ventures and others. The round values the consumer crypto trading app at more than seven times its raise, signaling continued investor appetite for retail-facing trading platforms despite a crowded brokerage field.

title: MoneyGram becomes Solana validator, expands blockchain payments strategy
blurb: MoneyGram became a validator on the Solana blockchain and joined the Solana Developer Platform, its third official validator deployment alongside Tempo and the Midnight Network. The move lets the payments firm stake SOL and process blocks while expanding a stablecoin strategy that already includes its MGUSD token, Stellar on- and off-ramps, and a recent Kraken off-ramp partnership.

title: Bank of Korea advances CBDC deposit token initiative to test real-world usage: report
blurb: The Bank of Korea is advancing its CBDC deposit token pilot to a second phase, integrating the tokens into commercial banks' core account systems for real-world transactions, settlements, and digital vouchers replacing government subsidies. The expansion contrasts with the US, where pending legislation would ban CBDC issuance through 2030 as the Trump administration focuses on private digital assets instead.

title: Spot bitcoin ETFs log sixth consecutive week of net outflows; analyst says selling wave is exhausting itself
blurb: US spot bitcoin ETFs recorded $227 million in net outflows last week, extending the negative-flow streak to six straight weeks. The persistent redemptions signal continued institutional caution, though one analyst argues the selling wave is losing momentum as the largest outflows appear to be behind the funds.

title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Buys $34.9 Million of Bitcoin Using Common Stock
blurb: Strategy bought $34.9M of Bitcoin, funding the purchase by selling common stock for the third consecutive week despite earlier pledges to shift toward perpetual preferred shares. Leaning on equity issuance during a flat tape pressures the stock's premium to net asset value, the metric that lets the company keep accumulating Bitcoin faster than it dilutes shareholders.

title: Geopolitical relief meets the Warsh Fed: Crypto Week Ahead
blurb: Bitcoin enters the week attempting to decouple from a shifting macro backdrop, with the Bank of Japan lifting rates to 1.0%, newly appointed Fed Chair Kevin Warsh restructuring the FOMC framework, and a signed US-Iran treaty reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Traders are positioning around Thursday's May Core PCE print, estimated at 3.3% YoY, as the SEC and CFTC open a 60-day joint comment window on derivatives reporting.

title: Bitcoin mining network becoming more sensitive to price swings, JPMorgan says
blurb: JPMorgan said Bitcoin mining difficulty has grown far more sensitive to price this year, with its beta to BTC moves climbing to 0.62 as roughly 20% of miners run unprofitable below the estimated $78,000 production cost. Publicly traded miners sold more than 32,000 BTC in Q1, exceeding all of 2025, and larger difficulty swings are likely if Bitcoin stays under cost.

title: Tom Lee says ‘best years for crypto remain ahead’ as Bitmine buys another 52,203 ETH
blurb: Bitmine purchased another 52,203 ETH, raising its treasury to 5.67 million ETH as chairman Tom Lee reiterated a long-term bullish outlook on crypto. The accumulation makes Bitmine one of the largest corporate holders of ETH, extending the public-company treasury strategy beyond Bitcoin into Ethereum.

title: Bitmine added $92 million of ETH, with Tom Lee continuing to believe in 'crypto spring'
blurb: Bitmine bought 52,203 ETH (roughly $92M) last week, lifting its treasury to 5.67M ETH worth nearly $10B and putting it at 4.7% of circulating supply, 94% of the way to its 5% target. Chairman Tom Lee said the firm will keep accumulating steadily through 2026, funded partly by $223M in projected annual staking revenue from the 83% of its holdings now staked.

title: Japan’s corporate pension fund plans to allocate 1% of assets to crypto: reports
blurb: Japan's National Business Corporate Pension Fund plans to allocate roughly 1% of its 21.3 billion yen ($131.8M) in assets to crypto in fiscal 2026, investing through a hedge-fund-managed passive fund. The move, framed as currency-risk diversification, lands as Japan advances a bill to classify crypto as financial instruments and its three megabanks prepare a jointly issued stablecoin.

title: Bank of England drops proposed holding caps for sterling stablecoins, sets £40 billion issuance guardrail
blurb: The Bank of England dropped proposed individual holding caps for sterling stablecoins, replacing them with a £40B systemic issuance guardrail and revised backing-asset rules ahead of a targeted 2027 launch. The shift removes a per-user constraint that issuers had argued would throttle adoption, signaling the BoE wants to scale UK stablecoin activity while capping aggregate systemic exposure.

title: Bank of England backs down on strict stablecoin holding limits, sets $50 billion issuance cap
blurb: The Bank of England abandoned its proposed £20,000 individual and £10M corporate stablecoin holding limits, replacing them with a temporary £40B ($50.6B) issuance cap on any single systemic stablecoin ahead of full U.K. crypto rules in 2027. Regulators also cut the non-interest-bearing reserve requirement to 30%, letting issuers park up to 70% of backing in short-term U.K. T-bills while still barring interest payments to holders.

title: TradFi fund manager Baillie Gifford introduces Solana, Ethereum tokenized fund with BNY
blurb: Baillie Gifford launched the Enhanced Yield Fund (BAGEY), a dollar-denominated tokenized fund of short-duration corporate bonds issued natively on Ethereum and Solana, with BNY providing tokenization and wallet infrastructure and NatWest acting as depositary. The fund yields around 7% and uses a U.K.-regulated OEIC structure where the blockchain is the register of record, giving eligible investors in the U.K., Switzerland, and Cayman Islands direct onchain ownership rather than a token wrapped over a legacy fund.

title: Polymarket paid creators to stage fake winning bets on dummy sites: WSJ
blurb: The Wall Street Journal reported that Polymarket paid influencers to film fake winning bets, with none of the roughly $1.9 million in wagers shown across more than 1,100 reviewed videos turning out to be real. The findings raise consumer-protection and disclosure questions for prediction markets as Polymarket pushes into the regulated US market following its CFTC-cleared reentry.

title: MoneyGram joins Solana as validator amid stablecoin payment push
blurb: MoneyGram became a validator on Solana, helping process and secure transactions on the proof-of-stake network as it builds out stablecoin-based payment infrastructure. The move follows the launch of its MGUSD stablecoin on Stellar via Stripe-owned Bridge and an anchor-validator role on Tempo, signaling a multi-chain strategy to position the remittance firm as infrastructure rather than just a user of stablecoin rails.

title: Bitcoin developers want to fix the 'replace this transaction with a higher fee' button. Here's why
blurb: Bitcoin Core developers are proposing to remove explicit replace-by-fee (RBF) signaling from wallet software, since full-RBF is now the network's default policy and the opt-in flag has become redundant. The legacy signal leaves an on-chain fingerprint that reveals which wallet sent a transaction, so developers are coordinating on a common default sequence number, likely the already dominant MAX-2, to make transactions across wallets harder to track.

title: Bitget enables direct US stock purchases using crypto
blurb: Bitget launched Stock+, letting users buy full and fractional US stocks funded directly from crypto balances and settled through regulated brokers. The feature folds equities into a crypto-exchange wallet without a separate brokerage transfer, extending the playbook of trading apps that have blurred the line between crypto and traditional securities access.

title: OKX and NYSE partner to bridge tradfi and crypto markets in joint venture led by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: OKX and Intercontinental Exchange, owner of the NYSE, formed a joint venture led by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo to give OKX's 120 million users access to ICE futures and NYSE tokenized equities, pending regulatory approval. The venture plans to operate as a registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, extending ICE's digital-asset push after its strategic investment in OKX at a $25B valuation.

title: South Korea’s Toss Bank to test blockchain-based financial infrastructure on Solana
blurb: Toss Bank, South Korea's internet-only bank serving 15 million customers, signed an MOU with the Solana Foundation to build blockchain-based financial infrastructure, starting with a stablecoin remittance proof-of-concept. The first phase tests technical feasibility for overseas transfers, with later phases adding overseas partners and KYC/AML verification, extending remittance rails that already cover 30 countries and seven currencies.

title: Tokenized RWA market cap rises 40% to top $51 billion as industry races to define equity tokenization model: Bernstein
blurb: Tokenized real-world assets reached a $51B market cap, up 40%, with tokenized equities growing 130% as issuers compete to set the dominant model, according to Bernstein. The race centers on how equity tokenization gets structured legally and operationally, a question that will determine which platforms control settlement and custody as traditional securities move on-chain.

title: Meta Taps New WhatsApp Boss as Part of $900 Million Investment
blurb: Meta is investing $900M in Indian fintech startup Cred and plans to install founder Kunal Shah as the new head of WhatsApp. The move ties Meta's payments ambitions to one of India's largest credit-card management platforms, positioning WhatsApp to deepen its push into UPI-based payments in a market where it has long sought a fintech foothold.

title: Meta invests $900M into Indian fintech Cred for a ~20% stake, and plans to appoint Cred founder Kunal Shah as the leader of WhatsApp, replacing Will Cathcart (Kurt Wagner/Bloomberg)
blurb: Meta invested $900M into Indian fintech Cred for a roughly 20% stake and plans to install Cred founder Kunal Shah as head of WhatsApp, replacing Will Cathcart. The move ties Meta's payments ambitions to Shah's consumer-credit playbook, signaling a renewed push to turn WhatsApp into a commerce and fintech platform in India, its largest market by users.

title: Crypto trading app Fomo raises $75 million at $550 million valuation in Index Ventures-led Series B
blurb: Fomo raised $75M at a $550M valuation in a Series B led by Index Ventures, with participation from Union Square Ventures and others. The round prices a crypto trading app well into unicorn-adjacent territory, signaling that investor appetite for retail-facing trading infrastructure is holding even as regulatory scrutiny of consumer crypto venues continues.

title: Cuomo to Lead Crypto Venture With New York Stock Exchange Owner
blurb: Andrew Cuomo will lead a venture backed by crypto exchange OKX and Intercontinental Exchange, the owner of the New York Stock Exchange, to build a business merging digital-asset and traditional financial markets. The pairing of a major crypto venue with an incumbent exchange operator signals deepening institutional convergence, putting established market infrastructure behind the integration of crypto trading and settlement with regulated rails.

title: Altura winds down stablecoin vault after ‘unprecedented level’ of withdrawal requests
blurb: Altura is winding down its stablecoin vault after what the firm called an unprecedented level of withdrawal requests, with its CEO attributing the run partly to market speculation tied to the depegging of Main Street's msUSD. The episode underscores how quickly contagion spreads across stablecoin yield products, where a single depeg can trigger redemption pressure that forces otherwise solvent vaults to unwind.

title: MoneyGram becomes Solana validator, expands blockchain payments strategy
blurb: MoneyGram became a validator on the Solana blockchain and joined the Solana Developer Platform, making Solana the third network where it runs an official validator alongside Tempo and Midnight. The move lets MoneyGram stake SOL and gives it access to platform tools for building payment products on Solana, deepening a blockchain strategy that already includes its MGUSD stablecoin and Stellar-based on- and off-ramps.

title: Bank of Korea advances CBDC deposit token initiative to test real-world usage: report
blurb: The Bank of Korea is advancing its CBDC pilot to a second phase, integrating deposit tokens into commercial banks' core account systems for real-world transactions, settlements, and digital vouchers replacing government subsidies. The move contrasts sharply with the US, where the Trump administration has pledged against a CBDC and a pending bill would ban issuance through 2030.

title: Spot bitcoin ETFs log sixth consecutive week of net outflows; analyst says selling wave is exhausting itself
blurb: US spot bitcoin ETFs recorded $227M in net outflows last week, extending the negative-flow streak to six straight weeks. The persistent withdrawals signal continued institutional caution, though one analyst argues the selling wave is exhausting itself as the weekly outflow pace moderates from prior weeks.

title: SpaceX makes bond sale days after record IPO, discloses over $100 billion cash pile
blurb: SpaceX launched its inaugural senior unsecured notes offering and disclosed roughly $100.8 billion in cash, days after a record IPO that valued the company past Amazon. Proceeds will retire bridge financing and fund its AI ambitions, including space-based data centers, while shares fell about 8% on the debt news after surging since the June 12 listing.

title: SpaceX Kicks Off Debut High-Grade Bond Sale
blurb: SpaceX launched its first investment-grade bond sale, raising debt to fund AI infrastructure following its record IPO. The move marks a shift for a company long financed by equity and private capital, signaling that high-grade debt markets are now open to it as it scales compute spending alongside its launch and Starlink businesses.

title: Robinhood Seeks to Raise $2 Billion in Convertible Bond Sale
blurb: Robinhood is seeking to raise $2B through a convertible bond offering, joining a wave of companies tapping the convertible market to pool cheap cash. The structure lets Robinhood raise capital at lower coupons than straight debt while deferring dilution until conversion, giving it a larger balance sheet to fund expansion across crypto, retirement, and lending without immediately pressuring its stock.

title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Buys $34.9 Million of Bitcoin Using Common Stock
blurb: Strategy bought $34.9M of Bitcoin funded by common stock sales, the third consecutive week it has tapped its at-the-market equity program despite earlier pledges to shift toward perpetual preferred shares. The repeated reliance on common stock signals continued dilution pressure on shareholders, and suggests the preferred-share pivot Saylor outlined is proving harder to execute than the equity route the company keeps falling back on.

title: Bitcoin mining network becoming more sensitive to price swings, JPMorgan says
blurb: JPMorgan said bitcoin's mining difficulty has grown sharply more sensitive to price this year, with its beta to BTC moves climbing to 0.62 as more miners run near breakeven below the estimated $78,000 production cost. With bitcoin trading under production cost for five straight months and roughly 20% of miners unprofitable, public miners sold over 32,000 BTC in Q1, exceeding their combined 2025 sales.

title: ‘Owning and controlling the entire AI stack is essential’: Europe is doubling down on its own AI infrastructure
blurb: European AI infrastructure startups have raised €1.9B this year, nearly matching all of 2025, as governments and regulated sectors push to own domestic compute rather than rely on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud, which control 70% of the European market. For finance and government buyers, the UK's £1.1bn AI plan and the EU's Tech Sovereignty Package signal that compute is now treated as critical infrastructure with national-security weight.

title: Hong Kong Seeks IPO Access for Chinese Investors, Higher Quotas
blurb: Hong Kong is in talks with mainland regulators to widen cross-border investment quotas and let Chinese investors buy into local IPOs, Financial Secretary Paul Chan said. The move follows a crackdown on illicit offshore capital flows and would channel mainland demand into Hong Kong's listing market, deepening liquidity for issuers choosing the city over New York.

title: SpaceX Declines for a Third Day After Announcing Bond Sale
blurb: SpaceX is selling investment-grade bonds for the first time, opening a debt-funded borrowing spree to bankroll its AI ambitions. Shares fell for a third straight day on the news, signaling investor unease about a capital-intensive company taking on leverage to chase compute alongside its core launch and Starlink businesses.

title: Chinese Backing Helps African Startup Spiro Near Unicorn Status
blurb: Chinese investor NewTrails Capital is putting $55M into African electric-mobility startup Spiro, pushing the company toward a $1B valuation. The deal underscores how Chinese capital is filling the funding gap left by Western investors in African fintech and mobility, where embedded financing for electric vehicles increasingly runs through pay-as-you-go and asset-backed lending models.

title: Bitmine added $92 million of ETH, with Tom Lee continuing to believe in 'crypto spring'
blurb: Bitmine bought 52,203 ETH last week for roughly $92 million, lifting its holdings to 5.67 million ETH worth nearly $10 billion, or 4.7% of circulating supply and 94% of the way to its 5% target. Chairman Tom Lee says the firm will keep accumulating through 2026, funding the strategy with 9.50% preferred shares and roughly $223 million in projected annual staking revenue from its 4.72 million staked ETH.

title: Why Meta wants CRED: The $4B fintech deal that could reshape India’s payments industry
blurb: Meta is reportedly in talks to invest tens of millions in Bengaluru-based CRED at a roughly $4B valuation, per Moneycontrol, in an exploratory deal that would give it a payments foothold in India. The move targets the missing piece in Meta's commerce stack across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, though PhonePe and Google Pay still control nearly 80% of UPI transactions.

title: Saudi Central Bank Said to Pull Money From at Least Two Managers
blurb: Saudi Arabia's central bank, SAMA, withdrew billions of dollars from at least two global asset managers in recent months, signaling that one of the world's largest pools of capital is becoming more selective in how it deploys money. The pullback raises competitive pressure across the asset-management industry, where sovereign mandates anchor revenue and a single redemption can reshape a firm's institutional flows.
